```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r load-and-split}
data <- read_csv("Stock.csv")

# Name each company's data
stock_list <- split(data, data$Company)
list2env(stock_list, envir = .GlobalEnv)
```

```{r summarize}
# Quick summary table
map_dfr(names(stock_list), function(nm) {
  df <- stock_list[[nm]]
  tibble(
    Company     = nm,
    Days        = nrow(df),
    `Avg Close` = round(mean(df$Close, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `Max High`  = round(max(df$High,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `Min Low`   = round(min(df$Low,   na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  )
})
```

```{r price, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
data |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = Close, colour = Company)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Closing Price Over Time",
       x = NULL, y = "Close ($)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r volume, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
data |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date),
         Month = floor_date(Date, "month")) |>
  group_by(Company, Month) |>
  summarise(Avg_Volume = mean(Volume / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = Avg_Volume, fill = Company)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Average Monthly Volume Traded",
       x = NULL, y = "Avg Volume (millions)", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r boxplot, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# Boxplot of closing price by company
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Company, y = Close, fill = Company)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Closing Prices by Company",
       x = NULL, y = "Close ($)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r distributions, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# Density distribution of closing prices
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Close, fill = Company)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Closing Price Distributions",
       x = "Close ($)", y = "Density", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r correlation-heatmap, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Correlation matrix per company (no extra packages needed)
for (nm in names(stock_list)) {
  cor_matrix <- stock_list[[nm]] |>
    select(Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) |>
    cor(use = "complete.obs")

  melted <- as.data.frame(cor_matrix) |>
    rownames_to_column("Var1") |>
    pivot_longer(-Var1, names_to = "Var2", values_to = "value")

  p <- ggplot(melted, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(colour = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#d73027", mid = "white", high = "#4575b4",
                         midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Correlation Matrix —", nm),
         x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "r") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  print(p)
}
```
